No. The EPM's weekend-rollover rule (Rule D-1) applies only to the response deadline, not to the compliance plan deadline. The deadline is August 31 as stated.

The proposed monitoring frequency was insufficient — the plan proposed monthly sampling but weekly sampling is required.
14 business days from the rejection date (September 15), per Rule D-4
October 3, 2025 (Friday)

14 business days counted from September 15 (not including September 15 itself), excluding weekends and Board holidays. September 16-19 (4 days), September 22-26 (9 days), September 29-30 (11 days), October 1-3 (14 days). No Board holidays fall within this window.
No. September 1 (Labor Day) is a Board holiday but it falls before the rejection date. No holidays fall within the September 16 – October 3 counting window.

September 29, 2025 (14 calendar days from the September 15 rejection, per Rule D-8)

Yes. The appeal deadline is October 3 and the appeal was filed September 22.
Yes. Executive Director Tan stated they are separate proceedings. The EPM does not explicitly address the overlap, but the Board will review the revised plan and the appeal on separate tracks.
No. The penalty assessment clock has not started.
Because Granger filed a timely response on July 17. Rule D-5 only activates the penalty clock if no response is filed by the response deadline.
The appeal tolls the penalty clock under Rule D-6, but the penalty clock is not running (since a timely response was filed), so tolling has no current practical effect.
October 9, 2025 (21 calendar days from the September 18 revised plan submission)
The revised plan is deemed provisionally approved under Rule D-3.
